pi_items.source: HTTP HEAD request confirmed HTTP 200 response
Screaming Frog documentation does not specify a default filename for near duplicate exports; filename may be user-assigned or tool-assigned

Official Screaming Frog documentation (https://www.screamingfrog.co.uk/seo-spider/user-guide/tabs/) consistently refers to 'Closest Similarity Match' as the column containing similarity percentages for near duplicates
Path is user-defined; no verification possible for this claim

privileged_info_summary: The task targets https://crawler-test.com/, a real public website. Data is not synthetic. The Screaming Frog SEO Spider version 23.2 is installed, which includes near duplicate detection using a minhash algorithm at 90% similarity threshold. The official Screaming Frog documentation identifies the similarity column as 'Closest Similarity Match', not 'Similarity' as claimed in metadata. The verifier script uses a case-insensitive grep for 'Similarity', which would match 'Closest Similarity Match' in the actual CSV export, so the verifier would pass despite the metadata discrepancy. The expected CSV filename is not verified in official documentation.
